web_text: Paul, a servant of Jesus Christ, called to be an apostle, set apart for the Good News of God, which he promised before through his prophets in the holy Scriptures, concerning his Son, who was born of the offspring of David according to the flesh, who was declared to be the Son of God with power, according to the Spirit of holiness, by the resurrection from the dead, Jesus Christ our Lord,
slang_text: This is Paul, totally a servant of Jesus Christ, called out and set apart special to preach God's Good News -- the stuff he already promised way back through his prophets in the holy Scriptures. It's all about his Son, who as a regular dude got born from David's line, but who got declared the Son of God with straight-up power, by the Holy Spirit, when he busted outta the grave alive again -- Jesus Christ our Lord, fer sure.

web_text: through whom we received grace and apostleship, for obedience of faith among all the nations, for his name’s sake; among whom you are also called to belong to Jesus Christ; to all who are in Rome, beloved of God, called to be saints: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ. First, I thank my God through Jesus Christ for all of you, that your faith is proclaimed throughout the whole world.
slang_text: Through him we scored grace and got the apostle gig, so we could get every nation to buckle down and trust him, all for his name's sake. And you Romans are part of that crew too, called to belong to Jesus Christ. To all you guys in Rome, God's beloved, called to be his saints: grace and peace to you from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ. First off, I gotta thank my God through Jesus Christ for every last one of you, 'cause word of your faith is bodacious news that's spreading clear across the whole world.

web_text: For God is my witness, whom I serve in my spirit in the Good News of his Son, how unceasingly I make mention of you always in my prayers, requesting, if by any means now at last I may be prospered by the will of God to come to you. For I long to see you, that I may impart to you some spiritual gift, to the end that you may be established; that is, that I with you may be encouraged in you, each of us by the other’s faith, both yours and mine.
slang_text: God's my witness on this, the one I serve with my whole spirit spreading the Good News about his Son -- I never stop bringing you up in my prayers, no joke. I keep asking that if God's willing, maybe I'll finally get to bounce on over and see you guys. 'Cause for real, I'm dying to see you, so I can hook you up with some spiritual gift that'll get you established and solid. Basically I want us to pump each other up -- your faith encouraging me, my faith encouraging you, a two-way deal.

web_text: Now I don’t desire to have you unaware, brothers, that I often planned to come to you, and was hindered so far, that I might have some fruit among you also, even as among the rest of the Gentiles. I am debtor both to Greeks and to foreigners, both to the wise and to the foolish. So, as much as is in me, I am eager to preach the Good News to you also who are in Rome. For I am not ashamed of the Good News of Christ, because it is the power of God for salvation for everyone who believes; for the Jew first, and also for the Greek.
slang_text: Now I don't want you guys trippin' and left in the dark, brothers -- I planned on coming to you tons of times, but kept getting blocked, so I could score some fruit among you too, same as with the rest of the Gentiles. I owe it to Greeks and to outsiders alike, to the smart ones and the dipsticks alike. So as much as it's up to me, I'm hyped to preach the Good News to you Rome dudes too. 'Cause no way am I ashamed of the Good News about Christ -- it's God's own power to save anybody who believes, the Jew first, then the Greek, fer sure.

web_text: For in it is revealed God’s righteousness from faith to faith. As it is written, “But the righteous shall live by faith.” For the wrath of God is revealed from heaven against all ungodliness and unrighteousness of men, who suppress the truth in unrighteousness, because that which is known of God is revealed in them, for God revealed it to them. For the invisible things of him since the creation of the world are clearly seen, being perceived through the things that are made, even his everlasting power and divinity; that they may be without excuse.
slang_text: 'Cause this Good News lays out God's righteousness straight up, faith from start to finish. Like it's written, "The righteous shall live by faith," no duh. But check it, God's wrath is also coming down loud from heaven against all the godless, bogus stuff people pull, folks who bury the truth under their own unrighteousness. See, what can be known about God is right there in plain sight for them, 'cause God himself showed it to them. Ever since the world got created, his invisible qualities -- his everlasting power, his total divine-ness -- have been totally obvious, seen clear as day through everything he made. So they got zero excuse, none, zip.

web_text: Because, knowing God, they didn’t glorify him as God, neither gave thanks, but became vain in their reasoning, and their senseless heart was darkened. Professing themselves to be wise, they became fools, and traded the glory of the incorruptible God for the likeness of an image of corruptible man, and of birds, and four-footed animals, and creeping things. Therefore God also gave them up in the lusts of their hearts to uncleanness, that their bodies should be dishonored among themselves;
slang_text: 'Cause even though they knew God, they didn't glorify him as God or even say thanks -- instead their thinking went totally bogus and their clueless hearts went dark. Claiming to be so wise, they turned into total wastoids, and traded the glory of the never-fading God for some grody knockoff image -- of a mortal man, or birds, or four-legged critters, or creepy-crawlies. So God let 'em have it, handed them over to the nasty cravings of their own hearts, straight into filth, till they were dishonoring their own bodies with each other.

web_text: who exchanged the truth of God for a lie, and worshiped and served the creature rather than the Creator, who is blessed forever. Amen. For this reason, God gave them up to vile passions. For their women changed the natural function into that which is against nature. Likewise also the men, leaving the natural function of the woman, burned in their lust toward one another, men doing what is inappropriate with men, and receiving in themselves the due penalty of their error. Even as they refused to have God in their knowledge, God gave them up to a reprobate mind, to do those things which are not fitting;
slang_text: They swapped God's truth for a straight-up lie, and started worshiping and serving the created stuff instead of the Creator, who's blessed forever, amen. That's exactly why God handed them over to nasty, vile passions. Their women swapped out the natural way for something against nature. Same deal with the men -- they ditched the natural way with women and burned with lust for each other, dudes doing stuff with dudes that's just not right, and copping the consequences for their own error right in their bodies. And since they blew off keeping God in their thinking, God gave them up to a messed-up, worthless mind, to go do stuff that's flat-out not okay --

web_text: being filled with all unrighteousness, sexual immorality, wickedness, covetousness, malice; full of envy, murder, strife, deceit, evil habits, secret slanderers, backbiters, hateful to God, insolent, haughty, boastful, inventors of evil things, disobedient to parents, without understanding, covenant breakers, without natural affection, unforgiving, unmerciful; who, knowing the ordinance of God, that those who practice such things are worthy of death, not only do the same, but also approve of those who practice them.
slang_text: They're packed to the gills with every kind of unrighteousness -- sexual immorality, wickedness, greed, straight-up malice. Loaded with envy, murder, fighting, lying, rotten habits, secret trash-talkers, backstabbers, haters of God, arrogant, stuck-up, braggy, always cooking up new evil, dissing their own parents, clueless, breaking every promise they make, no natural love for their own family, unforgiving, showing zero mercy. And here's the kicker -- they know full well God's rule, that people who pull this stuff deserve death, but they don't just keep doing it themselves, they cheer on everybody else who does it too. That's way harsh, and totally bogus.

web_text: Therefore you are without excuse, O man, whoever you are who judge. For in that which you judge another, you condemn yourself. For you who judge practice the same things. We know that the judgment of God is according to truth against those who practice such things. Do you think this, O man who judges those who practice such things, and do the same, that you will escape the judgment of God? Or do you despise the riches of his goodness, forbearance, and patience, not knowing that the goodness of God leads you to repentance?
slang_text: So dude, you've got zero excuse, whoever you are, if you're out there pointing fingers. 'Cause every time you judge somebody else, you're totally busting yourself, since you're pulling the same bogus moves. We already know God judges the real deal, straight up, on anybody who does this stuff. So what's your damage, thinking you can trash-talk people for doing what you do too, and just bounce right past God's judgment? Or are you dissin' how loaded he is with goodness, patience, and cutting you slack, not even clued in that God's kindness is what's supposed to walk you back to him in the first place?

web_text: But according to your hardness and unrepentant heart you are treasuring up for yourself wrath in the day of wrath, revelation, and of the righteous judgment of God; who “will pay back to everyone according to their works:” to those who by perseverance in well-doing seek for glory, honor, and incorruptibility, eternal life; but to those who are self-seeking, and don’t obey the truth, but obey unrighteousness, will be wrath and indignation,
slang_text: But 'cause your heart's hard as a rock and you won't cop to anything, you're basically stockpiling a truckload of wrath for yourself for that big day when God lays it all bare and judges everybody totally fair and square. He's "gonna pay everybody back exactly for what they did" -- no exceptions. Folks who keep grinding away doing good, chasing after glory, honor, and the kind that never fades, they score eternal life. But the wastoids who are all about themselves, who blow off the truth and cozy up to what's wrong instead, they're gonna catch some serious wrath and fury,

web_text: oppression and anguish, on every soul of man who does evil, to the Jew first, and also to the Greek. But glory, honor, and peace go to every man who does good, to the Jew first, and also to the Greek. For there is no partiality with God. For as many as have sinned without the law will also perish without the law. As many as have sinned under the law will be judged by the law.
slang_text: trouble and gut-wrenching pain, on every single soul out there doing grody stuff, Jew first and the Greek right behind. But glory, honor, and peace go out to everybody doing good, Jew first and the Greek too. 'Cause no duh, God doesn't play favorites. Whoever sinned without ever knowing the law is gonna get wiped out without the law judging 'em; whoever sinned while they had the law is gonna get judged by that same law.

web_text: For it isn’t the hearers of the law who are righteous before God, but the doers of the law will be justified (for when Gentiles who don’t have the law do by nature the things of the law, these, not having the law, are a law to themselves, in that they show the work of the law written in their hearts, their conscience testifying with them, and their thoughts among themselves accusing or else excusing them) in the day when God will judge the secrets of men, according to my Good News, by Jesus Christ.
slang_text: 'Cause just hearing the law read to you doesn't make you righteous before God -- it's the ones actually doing it who get called right with him. (Check it: when Gentiles who never even had the law naturally do what the law asks anyway, they're their own law, no rulebook needed. They're basically walking around with the law's work scribbled on their hearts, their conscience backing 'em up, and their own thoughts either busting 'em or letting 'em off the hook.) All this goes down on the day God judges everybody's hidden stuff, straight outta my Good News, through Jesus Christ.

web_text: Indeed you bear the name of a Jew, and rest on the law, and glory in God, and know his will, and approve the things that are excellent, being instructed out of the law, and are confident that you yourself are a guide of the blind, a light to those who are in darkness, a corrector of the foolish, a teacher of babies, having in the law the form of knowledge and of the truth.
slang_text: So okay, you're rockin' the name "Jew," leaning hard on the law, totally stoked on God, you know his will, and you can sniff out what's righteous 'cause you've been schooled in the law. You're way sure you're a guide for the blind, a light for whoever's stuck in the dark, a corrector for the spazzes, a teacher for the babies, since the law's handed you the whole outline of knowledge and truth.

web_text:
You therefore who teach another, don’t you teach yourself? You who preach that a man shouldn’t steal, do you steal? You who say a man shouldn’t commit adultery. Do you commit adultery? You who abhor idols, do you rob temples? You who glory in the law, do you dishonor God by disobeying the law? For “the name of God is blasphemed among the Gentiles because of you,”
 just as it is written.
slang_text: So you, dipstick, teaching everybody else -- how 'bout teaching yourself for once? You preach nobody should steal, but are you swiping stuff on the side? You say nobody should cheat on their spouse, but are you out there cheating? You gag at idols, but are you raiding temples for cash? You're so stoked on the law, but do you dis God by breaking it anyway? 'Cause like it says, "God's name gets straight-up trashed among the Gentiles 'cause of you clowns," just like it's written.

web_text: For circumcision indeed profits, if you are a doer of the law, but if you are a transgressor of the law, your circumcision has become uncircumcision. If therefore the uncircumcised keep the ordinances of the law, won’t his uncircumcision be accounted as circumcision? Won’t the uncircumcision which is by nature, if it fulfills the law, judge you, who with the letter and circumcision are a transgressor of the law? For he is not a Jew who is one outwardly, neither is that circumcision which is outward in the flesh;
slang_text: Fer sure, circumcision's worth something if you're actually doing the law, but if you're breaking it, your circumcision might as well not even count -- totally bogus. So flip it around: if some uncircumcised dude keeps the law's rules, isn't he gonna get counted as circumcised anyway? And won't that guy who's uncircumcised by nature but still pulls off the law totally show you up -- you with the letter of the law and the circumcision, still out here breaking it? 'Cause being a real Jew ain't about what shows on the outside, and real circumcision ain't just some skin-deep thing;

web_text: but he is a Jew who is one inwardly, and circumcision is that of the heart, in the spirit not in the letter; whose praise is not from men, but from God.
slang_text: nah, the real Jew is the one who's it on the inside, and the real circumcision's a heart thing, done up by the Spirit, not some rulebook -- and that dude ain't lookin' for props from people, he's after praise from God himself.

web_text:
Then what advantage does the Jew have? Or what is the profit of circumcision? Much in every way! Because first of all, they were entrusted with the revelations of God. For what if some were without faith? Will their lack of faith nullify the faithfulness of God? May it never be! Yes, let God be found true, but every man a liar. As it is written,
“That you might be justified in your words,
and might prevail when you come into judgment.”
slang_text:
So what's the deal with being Jewish, then? Is there any perk to gettin' circumcised? Totally, big time, in like every way. First off, they're the ones God trusted with His own righteous words -- straight up. So what if some of 'em bailed on the faith thing? Does their bailing mean God stops keepin' His word? No way, dude, don't even go there. Let God be for real even if every single human turns out to be a liar. Like it says in the Psalms:
"So you'll be proven right in what you say,
and you'll come out on top when they haul you into court."

web_text: But if our unrighteousness commends the righteousness of God, what will we say? Is God unrighteous who inflicts wrath? I speak like men do. May it never be! For then how will God judge the world? For if the truth of God through my lie abounded to his glory, why am I also still judged as a sinner? Why not (as we are slanderously reported, and as some affirm that we say), “Let us do evil, that good may come?” Those who say so are justly condemned.
slang_text: But hold up -- if our bogus behavior actually makes God's righteousness look more radical, what's that say? Is God bein' unfair when He dishes out the smackdown? (I'm just talkin' like people do here.) No way, don't even trip. 'Cause if God wasn't fair, how could He judge the whole world? And if my lying somehow makes God's truth shine brighter and gets Him more glory, why am I still getting nailed as a sinner? Might as well say, "let's do grody stuff so good stuff happens" -- which, by the way, is exactly what some jokers claim we teach. Those dudes deserve whatever they get, fer sure.

web_text:
What then? Are we better than they? No, in no way. For we previously warned both Jews and Greeks, that they are all under sin. As it is written,
“There is no one righteous;
no, not one. There is no one who understands.
There is no one who seeks after God. They have all turned away.
They have together become unprofitable.
There is no one who does good,
no, not so much as one.”
slang_text:
So what's the verdict? Are we somehow better than them? No duh, not even close. We already laid it out -- Jews, Greeks, everybody's stuck under sin's thumb, no exceptions. Just like it's written:
"Nobody's righteous,
not one single dude.
Nobody's got a clue.
Nobody's even lookin' for God. Everybody bailed and went their own way.
The whole crew turned out totally useless.
Nobody's doin' good,
not even one."

web_text:
“Their throat is an open tomb.
With their tongues they have used deceit.”
“The poison of vipers is under their lips”; “whose mouth is full of cursing and bitterness.” “Their feet are swift to shed blood. Destruction and misery are in their ways.
slang_text:
"Their throat's like an open grave -- grody to the max.
Their tongues are straight-up cons."
"There's snake venom hidin' under their lips"; "their mouths are packed with cussin' and bitterness." "Their feet booked it fast whenever there was blood to spill. Wreckage and misery trail behind everywhere they go.

web_text: The way of peace, they haven’t known.” “There is no fear of God before their eyes.” Now we know that whatever things the law says, it speaks to those who are under the law, that every mouth may be closed, and all the world may be brought under the judgment of God. Because by the works of the law, no flesh will be justified in his sight. For through the law comes the knowledge of sin.
slang_text: They never even found the road to peace." "They got zero fear of God in their eyes, not one bit." Now here's the deal -- whatever the law says, it's talkin' to the folks livin' under the law, so that every mouth gets shut and the whole world stands guilty before God. 'Cause nobody gets declared righteous by pulling off the works of the law -- no way, dude. The law's whole job is just showin' you how grody your sin actually is.

web_text: But now apart from the law, a righteousness of God has been revealed, being testified by the law and the prophets; even the righteousness of God through faith in Jesus Christ to all and on all those who believe. For there is no distinction, for all have sinned, and fall short of the glory of God; being justified freely by his grace through the redemption that is in Christ Jesus;
slang_text: But check this out -- totally apart from the law, God's righteousness just got revealed, and it's the real deal, backed up by the Law and the Prophets themselves. It's God's righteousness that comes through trustin' in Jesus Christ, for everybody, on everybody who believes -- 'cause there's no difference between anybody here. Every last one of us blew it and fell way short of God's glory, no exceptions. And yet we get declared righteous, free of charge, by His grace, through the buyback that happens in Christ Jesus.

web_text: whom God sent to be an atoning sacrifice, through faith in his blood, for a demonstration of his righteousness through the passing over of prior sins, in God’s forbearance; to demonstrate his righteousness at this present time; that he might himself be just, and the justifier of him who has faith in Jesus. Where then is the boasting? It is excluded. By what kind of law? Of works? No, but by a law of faith. We maintain therefore that a man is justified by faith apart from the works of the law.
slang_text: God sent Him out to be the sacrifice that covers it all, through trustin' in His blood -- provin' God's righteousness, since He'd been holdin' back and lettin' sins slide before, showin' now, in this very moment, that He's righteous, so He can be totally fair AND the one who declares righteous anybody who trusts in Jesus. So where's the braggin' rights at? Outta here, gone, bounced. On what grounds -- doin' good works? Nope. It's the faith angle, dude. Bottom line: a person gets declared righteous by faith, not by grindin' out the works of the law.

web_text: Or is God the God of Jews only? Isn’t he the God of Gentiles also? Yes, of Gentiles also, since indeed there is one God who will justify the circumcised by faith, and the uncircumcised through faith. Do we then nullify the law through faith? May it never be! No, we establish the law.
slang_text: Or is God only the God of the Jewish crew? Isn't He the Gentiles' God too? Fer sure He is -- since there's only one God, and He's gonna declare the circumcised righteous by faith, and the uncircumcised righteous through that same faith. So are we tossin' out the law by leanin' on faith? No way, don't even go there. Real talk -- we're backin' the law up, like a boss.

web_text: What then will we say that Abraham, our forefather, has found according to the flesh? For if Abraham was justified by works, he has something to boast about, but not toward God. For what does the Scripture say? “Abraham believed God, and it was accounted to him for righteousness.” Now to him who works, the reward is not counted as grace, but as something owed.
slang_text: So okay, what's the deal with Abraham, our main man, our forefather? What'd he score by grinding it out on his own steam? 'Cause check it -- if Abraham got right with God by racking up good works, sure, he'd have some serious bragging rights. But not in front of God, no way, that's bogus logic. What does Scripture actually say? "Abraham believed God, and that got counted as righteousness." No duh -- when a dude's working for his paycheck, that paycheck isn't some free gift, it's just what he's owed, plain and simple.

web_text:
But to him who doesn’t work, but believes in him who justifies the ungodly, his faith is accounted for righteousness. Even as David also pronounces blessing on the man to whom God counts righteousness apart from works, “Blessed are they whose iniquities are forgiven,
whose sins are covered. Blessed is the man whom the Lord will by no means charge with sin.”
slang_text:
But for the dude who's not grinding out works, who's just totally trusting the God who makes even the grody, ungodly folks right with him -- that trust gets counted as righteousness, fer sure. David lays down the same bodacious truth, pronouncing a blessing on the guy God calls righteous apart from any works: "Blessed are the ones whose screwups get wiped clean,
whose sins get covered up for good. Blessed is the guy the Lord will never, ever pin sin on."

web_text: Is this blessing then pronounced on the circumcised, or on the uncircumcised also? For we say that faith was accounted to Abraham for righteousness. How then was it counted? When he was in circumcision, or in uncircumcision? Not in circumcision, but in uncircumcision. He received the sign of circumcision, a seal of the righteousness of the faith which he had while he was in uncircumcision, that he might be the father of all those who believe, though they might be in uncircumcision, that righteousness might also be accounted to them. He is the father of circumcision to those who not only are of the circumcision, but who also walk in the steps of that faith of our father Abraham, which he had in uncircumcision.
slang_text: So is that blessing only for the circumcised crew, or does it cover the uncircumcised folks too? 'Cause here's the thing -- we're saying Abraham's faith got counted as righteousness. So when'd that actually happen -- before he got circumcised, or after? Not after, dudes -- before! He got the circumcision thing later as a sign, like a stamp of approval sealing the righteousness he already had by faith while he was still uncircumcised. That way he could be the father of everybody who believes, even if they never get circumcised, and righteousness gets counted to them too, no duh. He's the father of the circumcised crowd too, but only the ones who don't just have the physical mark -- they gotta be walking the same faith-path our father Abraham blazed back when he was still uncircumcised.

web_text: For the promise to Abraham and to his offspring that he should be heir of the world wasn’t through the law, but through the righteousness of faith. For if those who are of the law are heirs, faith is made void, and the promise is made of no effect. For the law produces wrath, for where there is no law, neither is there disobedience. For this cause it is of faith, that it may be according to grace, to the end that the promise may be sure to all the offspring, not to that only which is of the law, but to that also which is of the faith of Abraham, who is the father of us all.
slang_text: See, the promise to Abraham and his whole family line, that he'd inherit the entire world, didn't come through keeping the law -- it came through the righteousness you get from faith. 'Cause if the law-keepers are the only heirs, then faith's totally pointless and the promise is bogus, dead in the water. The law just stirs up God's wrath, dudes -- where there's no law, there's nothin' to break in the first place. That's why it's gotta run on faith, so it lines up with grace, and so the promise stands solid for the whole family, not just the law-crowd but everybody who's got the same faith Abraham had. He's the father of us all, fer sure.

web_text: As it is written, “I have made you a father of many nations.” This is in the presence of him whom he believed: God, who gives life to the dead, and calls the things that are not, as though they were. Besides hope, Abraham in hope believed, to the end that he might become a father of many nations, according to that which had been spoken, “So will your offspring be.” Without being weakened in faith, he didn’t consider his own body, already having been worn out, (he being about a hundred years old), and the deadness of Sarah’s womb. Yet, looking to the promise of God, he didn’t waver through unbelief, but grew strong through faith, giving glory to God,
slang_text: Just like it's written: "I've made you the father of a whole bunch of nations." That's straight from the God he believed in -- the God who brings dead stuff back to life and calls things that don't even exist yet like they're already here, totally wild. When there was zero reason left to hope, Abraham hoped anyway and believed, so he could become the father of many nations, just like it was promised: "That's how many offspring you'll have." His faith never went weak, even though, let's be real, his own body was pretty worn out by then (the dude was pushing a hundred) and Sarah's womb was as good as dead. But he kept his eyes on God's promise instead of trippin' on doubt -- he got stronger in faith and gave God the glory,

web_text: and being fully assured that what he had promised, he was also able to perform. Therefore it also was “credited to him for righteousness.” Now it was not written that it was accounted to him for his sake alone, but for our sake also, to whom it will be accounted, who believe in him who raised Jesus, our Lord, from the dead,
slang_text: totally convinced that whatever God promised, God could straight-up deliver, no sweat. That's why it got "credited to him as righteousness." And that line wasn't just written for Abraham's sake alone -- it's for us too, dudes. It'll get credited to us the same way, to anybody who believes in the God who raised Jesus our Lord from the dead,

web_text: who was delivered up for our trespasses, and was raised for our justification.
slang_text: who got handed over to die for our screwups, and got raised back up so we could be made right with God. Righteous, no doubt about it.

web_text: Being therefore justified by faith, we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ; through whom we also have our access by faith into this grace in which we stand. We rejoice in hope of the glory of God. Not only this, but we also rejoice in our sufferings, knowing that suffering produces perseverance; and perseverance, proven character; and proven character, hope:
slang_text: So here's the deal -- since we're all squared away with God through faith, we've got peace with Him through our main man Jesus Christ. It's through Jesus that we even got a way in, into this bodacious grace we're standing on right now. We're stoked, totally rejoicing in the hope of God's glory. And it doesn't stop there -- we're even psyched about our hard times, 'cause we know rough patches build up perseverance, perseverance builds proven character, and proven character builds hope.

web_text: and hope doesn’t disappoint us, because God’s love has been poured out into our hearts through the Holy Spirit who was given to us. For while we were yet weak, at the right time Christ died for the ungodly. For one will hardly die for a righteous man. Yet perhaps for a righteous person someone would even dare to die. But God commends his own love toward us, in that while we were yet sinners, Christ died for us.
slang_text: And that hope's not gonna leave us hanging, 'cause God's love got poured straight into our hearts through the Holy Spirit, who He gave us fer sure. Check it -- while we were still total wastoids, weak and helpless, right on schedule Christ died for the ungodly. I mean, hardly anybody's gonna die for some upright dude. Maybe, maybe, somebody might work up the nerve to die for a genuinely righteous person. But God's straight-up showing off His own love for us: while we were still sinners, straight-up grody and messed up, Christ died for us. That's the real deal.

web_text: Much more then, being now justified by his blood, we will be saved from God’s wrath through him. For if, while we were enemies, we were reconciled to God through the death of his Son, much more, being reconciled, we will be saved by his life. Not only so, but we also rejoice in God through our Lord Jesus Christ, through whom we have now received the reconciliation. Therefore as sin entered into the world through one man, and death through sin; and so death passed to all men, because all sinned.

web_text: For Moses writes about the righteousness of the law, “The one who does them will live by them.” But the righteousness which is of faith says this, “Don’t say in your heart, ‘Who will ascend into heaven?’ (that is, to bring Christ down); or, ‘Who will descend into the abyss?’ (that is, to bring Christ up from the dead.)” But what does it say? “The word is near you, in your mouth, and in your heart”; that is, the word of faith, which we preach:

web_text: Even so then at this present time also there is a remnant according to the election of grace. And if by grace, then it is no longer of works; otherwise grace is no longer grace. slang_text: David lays it down like this: "Let their dinner table turn into a snare and a trap, a stumbling block that trips 'em up and pays 'em back. slang_text: So yeah, you gotta play it straight and get in line -- not just 'cause you're scared of getting smacked down, but 'cause your own conscience knows what's up. If therefore we live or die, we are the Lord’s.
slang_text: One dude thinks one day's way more righteous than the rest. Another dude thinks every day's basically the same, no biggie. Whatever -- everybody's gotta be totally chill and sure in their own head about it. The guy who makes a big deal outta one day, he's doin' it for the Lord. And the guy who doesn't, he's skippin' it for the Lord too. Same with chow -- the guy who eats does it for the Lord, 'cause he's thankin' God for the grub. And the guy who don't eat, he's also doin' it for the Lord, and he's thankin' God right along with him. Bottom line: none of us are just out here livin' for ourselves, and none of us just up and die for ourselves either. If we're livin', we're livin' for the Lord. If we're dyin', we're dyin' for the Lord. So whether we're livin' or croakin', dude, we belong to the Lord, fer sure.

web_text:
For to this end Christ died, rose, and lived again, that he might be Lord of both the dead and the living. But you, why do you judge your brother? Or you again, why do you despise your brother? For we will all stand before the judgment seat of Christ. For it is written,
“‘As I live,’ says the Lord, ‘to me every knee will bow.
Every tongue will confess to God.’” So then each one of us will give account of himself to God.
slang_text: See, this is exactly why Christ croaked and came back to life -- so he could be Lord over both the dead and the living, epic as that sounds. So what's your damage, judgin' your brother? Or you over there, why you dissin' your brother like that? We're all gonna stand before Christ's judgment seat someday, no way around it. 'Cause it's written down: "'As sure as I'm alive,' says the Lord, 'every knee's gonna bow to me. Every tongue's gonna confess to God.'" So don't go trippin' -- every single one of us is gonna have to explain ourselves to God, straight up.

web_text: Therefore let’s not judge one another any more, but judge this rather, that no man put a stumbling block in his brother’s way, or an occasion for falling. I know, and am persuaded in the Lord Jesus, that nothing is unclean of itself; except that to him who considers anything to be unclean, to him it is unclean. Yet if because of food your brother is grieved, you walk no longer in love. Don’t destroy with your food him for whom Christ died. Then don’t let your good be slandered,
slang_text: So let's quit judgin' each other, already -- instead, chew on this: don't go puttin' some stumbling block or trip-wire in your brother's path. I know it, and I'm totally convinced in the Lord Jesus, that nothin' is grody just by itself -- except if some dude thinks somethin's grody, well then to him it's grody, that's just how it works. But if your brother's gettin' bummed out over food, you're not walkin' in love no more, dude. Don't wreck somebody with your food when Christ died for that guy! So don't let your good thing get talked trash about,

web_text: for God’s Kingdom is not eating and drinking, but righteousness, peace, and joy in the Holy Spirit. For he who serves Christ in these things is acceptable to God and approved by men. So then, let us follow after things which make for peace, and things by which we may build one another up. Don’t overthrow God’s work for food’s sake. All things indeed are clean, however it is evil for that man who creates a stumbling block by eating.
slang_text: 'cause God's Kingdom ain't about what you're eatin' or drinkin' -- it's about righteousness, peace, and joy in the Holy Spirit, and that's the righteous stuff that actually matters. Whoever serves Christ like that is totally acceptable to God and gives off a good vibe to people too. So let's chase after whatever keeps the peace, and whatever builds each other up instead of tearin' each other down. Don't go bulldozin' God's work over some food beef. Sure, everything's technically clean, but it's bogus for a guy to make somebody else stumble just 'cause of what he's eatin'.

web_text: It is good to not eat meat, drink wine, nor do anything by which your brother stumbles, is offended, or is made weak. Do you have faith? Have it to yourself before God. Happy is he who doesn’t judge himself in that which he approves. But he who doubts is condemned if he eats, because it isn’t of faith; and whatever is not of faith is sin. Now to him who is able to establish you according to my Good News and the preaching of Jesus Christ, according to the revelation of the mystery which has been kept secret through long ages,
slang_text: It's a righteous move to skip the meat, skip the wine, skip whatever's gonna make your brother trip up, get offended, or go weak in the knees. You got faith about it? Cool, keep that between you and God. Blessed is the dude who doesn't gotta beat himself up over what he's already decided is fine. But the guy who's got doubts and eats anyway, he's condemned, 'cause that ain't comin' from faith -- and anything that's not outta faith is straight-up sin, no way around it. Now, to the One who's totally got the power to make you solid, accordin' to my Good News and the preachin' about Jesus Christ, accordin' to the mystery that's been kept under wraps since way, way back,

web_text: but now is revealed, and by the Scriptures of the prophets, according to the commandment of the eternal God, is made known for obedience of faith to all the nations; to the only wise God, through Jesus Christ, to whom be the glory forever! Amen.
slang_text: but now it's all out in the open, laid out through the Scriptures written by the prophets, straight from the eternal God's own command, so every nation on the map could get hip to it and obey through faith -- to the one and only wise God, through Jesus Christ, to him be the glory forever and ever! Amen, dudes.

web_text: Now we who are strong ought to bear the weaknesses of the weak, and not to please ourselves. Let each one of us please his neighbor for that which is good, to be building him up. For even Christ didn’t please himself. But, as it is written, “The reproaches of those who reproached you fell on me.” For whatever things were written before were written for our learning, that through perseverance and through encouragement of the Scriptures we might have hope.
slang_text: Okay, us strong dudes gotta carry the weak ones' baggage, not just kick back and please ourselves, no way. Each of us oughta look out for our neighbor, doing what's good, building 'em up like a boss. 'Cause even Christ didn't just please himself -- as it's written, "The trash talk aimed at you landed on me instead." Everything written way back when got written for our learning, so that through sticking it out and getting hyped up by the Scriptures, we could keep our hope totally alive.

web_text: Now the God of perseverance and of encouragement grant you to be of the same mind one with another according to Christ Jesus, that with one accord you may with one mouth glorify the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ. Therefore accept one another, even as Christ also accepted you, to the glory of God. Now I say that Christ has been made a servant of the circumcision for the truth of God, that he might confirm the promises given to the fathers,
slang_text: Now may the God who gives us the guts to hang tough and the hype to keep going, hook you all up with the same mindset toward each other, straight outta Christ Jesus -- so that together, with one voice, you can totally glorify the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ. So accept each other, dudes, just like Christ accepted you, for God's glory. Here's the deal: Christ became a servant to the circumcised crowd to show God's for-real, so he could lock in the promises made to the ancestors,

web_text:
and that the Gentiles might glorify God for his mercy. As it is written,
“Therefore will I give praise to you among the Gentiles,
and sing to your name.” Again he says,
“Rejoice, you Gentiles, with his people.” Again,
“Praise the Lord, all you Gentiles!
Let all the peoples praise him.” Again, Isaiah says,
“There will be the root of Jesse,
he who arises to rule over the Gentiles;
in him the Gentiles will hope.”
slang_text:
And that means us Gentiles get to glorify God too for his righteous mercy. Like it's written,
"So I'm gonna give you props among the Gentiles,
and sing out your name, totally."
And again he says,
"Party on, you Gentiles, right along with his people!"
And again,
"Praise the Lord, all you Gentiles!
Let every last one of the peoples praise him!"
And Isaiah chimes in too,
"There's gonna be a root from Jesse,
the dude who rises up to rule over the Gentiles;
and in him, the Gentiles are gonna put their hope, fer sure."

web_text: Now may the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in believing, that you may abound in hope, in the power of the Holy Spirit. I myself am also persuaded about you, my brothers, that you yourselves are full of goodness, filled with all knowledge, able also to admonish others. But I write the more boldly to you in part, as reminding you, because of the grace that was given to me by God, that I should be a servant of Christ Jesus to the Gentiles, serving as a priest of the Good News of God, that the offering up of the Gentiles might be made acceptable, sanctified by the Holy Spirit.
slang_text: Now may the God of hope fill you up totally with joy and peace as you keep believing, so you're overflowing with hope by the power of the Holy Spirit. I'm personally convinced about you guys, my brothers -- you're loaded with goodness, packed with all kinds of knowledge, and totally capable of straightening each other out. But I wrote to you kinda bold in spots, just remindin' you, on account of the grace God gave me to be a servant of Christ Jesus to the Gentiles, serving like a priest of the Good News of God, so that the Gentiles being offered up would be legit and acceptable, made righteous by the Holy Spirit.

web_text: I have therefore my boasting in Christ Jesus in things pertaining to God. For I will not dare to speak of any things except those which Christ worked through me, for the obedience of the Gentiles, by word and deed, in the power of signs and wonders, in the power of God’s Spirit; so that from Jerusalem, and around as far as to Illyricum, I have fully preached the Good News of Christ; yes, making it my aim to preach the Good News, not where Christ was already named, that I might not build on another’s foundation.
slang_text: So I got my bragging rights in Christ Jesus when it comes to God's stuff. I ain't even gonna dare talk about anything except what Christ pulled off through me, getting the Gentiles to obey, by word and deed, with epic signs and wonders, by the power of God's Spirit -- so that all the way from Jerusalem, clear around to Illyricum, I've fully preached the Good News of Christ. Yeah, I made it my whole aim to preach the Good News where Christ wasn't already known, so I wouldn't be building on somebody else's foundation, no duh.

web_text:
But, as it is written,
“They will see, to whom no news of him came.
They who haven’t heard will understand.” Therefore also I was hindered these many times from coming to you, but now, no longer having any place in these regions, and having these many years a longing to come to you, whenever I travel to Spain, I will come to you. For I hope to see you on my journey, and to be helped on my way there by you, if first I may enjoy your company for a while.
slang_text:
But like it's written,
"Those who never got the news about him are gonna see it.
Those who never heard are gonna get it."
That's why I kept getting held up, tons of times, from coming to see you guys. But now, since there's no more turf left for me in these parts, and I've been jonesing for years to come check you out, whenever I bounce for Spain, I'm gonna swing by you. I'm hoping to see you on my way through, and get some help from you for the trip, once I've enjoyed hanging with your crew for a bit.

web_text: But now, I say, I am going to Jerusalem, serving the saints. For it has been the good pleasure of Macedonia and Achaia to make a certain contribution for the poor among the saints who are at Jerusalem. Yes, it has been their good pleasure, and they are their debtors. For if the Gentiles have been made partakers of their spiritual things, they owe it to them also to serve them in fleshly things. When therefore I have accomplished this, and have sealed to them this fruit, I will go on by way of you to Spain.
slang_text: But right now, I'm heading to Jerusalem, serving the saints. See, Macedonia and Achaia thought it'd be totally righteous to pass the hat for the poor saints over in Jerusalem. Yeah, they were stoked to do it, and honestly, they owe it to 'em. 'Cause if us Gentiles got in on their spiritual blessings, we oughta pay it back by helping 'em out with material stuff too, no duh. So once I've wrapped this up and made sure they got their fruit locked in, I'm gonna swing by your place on my way out to Spain.

web_text: I know that, when I come to you, I will come in the fullness of the blessing of the Good News of Christ. Now I beg you, brothers, by our Lord Jesus Christ, and by the love of the Spirit, that you strive together with me in your prayers to God for me, that I may be delivered from those who are disobedient in Judea, and that my service which I have for Jerusalem may be acceptable to the saints; that I may come to you in joy through the will of God, and together with you, find rest.
slang_text: I know that when I finally roll up to you, I'm gonna come totally loaded with the blessing of Christ's Good News. So I'm begging you, brothers, by our Lord Jesus Christ and by the love of the Spirit, throw down with me in some serious prayer to God on my behalf -- that I'd get delivered from the wastoids in Judea who won't get with the program, and that my service for Jerusalem would go over good with the saints; so I can come to you all stoked, by God's will, and finally kick back and find some rest together with you guys.

web_text: Now the God of peace be with you all. Amen.
slang_text: So may the God of peace chill with all of you. Amen, dudes.

web_text: I commend to you Phoebe, our sister, who is a servant of the assembly that is at Cenchreae, that you receive her in the Lord, in a way worthy of the saints, and that you assist her in whatever matter she may need from you, for she herself also has been a helper of many, and of my own self. Greet Prisca and Aquila, my fellow workers in Christ Jesus, who for my life, laid down their own necks; to whom not only I give thanks, but also all the assemblies of the Gentiles.
slang_text: Okay, gotta give you the scoop on Phoebe, our sister -- she's a righteous helper over at the assembly in Cenchreae. Roll out the welcome mat for her like she's family, the way saints oughta, and hook her up with whatever she needs, 'cause this dudette has been there for a ton of people, me included, no lie. Oh, and say hey to Priscilla and Aquila, my crew in Christ Jesus -- these two straight up risked their necks to save my life. I owe 'em big, and so does every Gentile assembly out there, fer sure.

web_text: Greet the assembly that is in their house. Greet Epaenetus, my beloved, who is the first fruits of Achaia to Christ. Greet Mary, who labored much for us. Greet Andronicus and Junia, my relatives and my fellow prisoners, who are notable among the apostles, who were also in Christ before me. Greet Amplias, my beloved in the Lord.
slang_text: Say what's up to the whole crew that meets over at their crib. Give a shoutout to Epaenetus, my main man, the very first dude in Achaia to get down with Christ -- totally righteous. Tell Mary hey too, she's worked her tail off for us. And give props to Andronicus and Junia, my own relatives who did time in the slammer right alongside me -- these two are legit big deal apostles, in Christ way before I ever was. Say hi to Amplias, my beloved bro in the Lord.

web_text: Greet Urbanus, our fellow worker in Christ, and Stachys, my beloved. Greet Apelles, the approved in Christ. Greet those who are of the household of Aristobulus. Greet Herodion, my kinsman. Greet them of the household of Narcissus, who are in the Lord. Greet Tryphaena and Tryphosa, who labor in the Lord. Greet Persis, the beloved, who labored much in the Lord.
slang_text: Give a wave to Urbanus, our teammate in Christ, and my main man Stachys. Say hey to Apelles, a dude who's proven himself totally solid in Christ. Give props to whoever's in Aristobulus's household. Shout out to Herodion, my own kin. Say what's up to the Narcissus household folks who are in the Lord. Give a holler to Tryphaena and Tryphosa, who bust it hard in the Lord's work. And say hi to Persis, my beloved, another dudette who's worked hella hard in the Lord.

web_text:
Greet Rufus, the chosen in the Lord, and his mother and mine. Greet Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, Hermas, and the brothers
 who are with them. Greet Philologus and Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas, and all the saints who are with them. Greet one another with a holy kiss. The assemblies of Christ greet you.
slang_text: Say hey to Rufus, a straight-up chosen dude in the Lord, and to his mom, who's basically been like a mom to me too. Give a shoutout to Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, Hermas, and all the brothers rolling with them. Say what's up to Philologus and Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas, plus every last saint hanging with them. Greet each other with a holy kiss, none of that grody fake stuff, the real deal. And hey, all the assemblies of Christ send their love back to you.

web_text:
Now I beg you, brothers, look out for those who are causing the divisions and occasions of stumbling, contrary to the doctrine which you learned, and turn away from them. For those who are such don’t serve our Lord, Jesus Christ, but their own belly; and by their smooth and flattering speech, they deceive the hearts of the innocent. For your obedience has become known to all. I rejoice therefore over you. But I desire to have you wise in that which is good, but innocent in that which is evil. And the God of peace will quickly crush Satan under your feet.
The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
slang_text: Now listen up, I'm begging you, bros -- keep your eyes peeled for the dipsticks stirring up drama and tripping people up, going totally against what you were taught. Bounce on outta there, steer clear of 'em. 'Cause those spazoids ain't serving our Lord Jesus Christ, they're just serving their own stomachs, and with all that smooth-talking, flattering nonsense they're straight up conning innocent folks. Now everybody's heard how you guys keep it obedient, and dude, that makes me stoked for you. But I want you wise about what's good, and totally clueless about evil stuff -- don't even go there. And get this: the God of peace is gonna crush Satan under your feet real soon, like a boss. Oh, and grace from our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.

web_text: Timothy, my fellow worker, greets you, as do Lucius, Jason, and Sosipater, my relatives. I, Tertius, who write the letter, greet you in the Lord. Gaius, my host and host of the whole assembly, greets you. Erastus, the treasurer of the city, greets you, as does Quartus, the brother. The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all! Amen.
slang_text: Timothy, my ride-or-die teammate, sends his hellos, and so do Lucius, Jason, and Sosipater, my own relatives. And hey, it's me, Tertius, the dude actually scribbling down this letter -- I'm sending you my own hey-there in the Lord. Gaius, who's putting me up and hosting the whole assembly, says hi. Erastus, the city's money guy, sends his greetings, and so does Quartus, our brother. May the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with each and every one of you! Amen, fer sure.
